tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and low total CO₂, suggesting impaired renal function with possible tubular involvement and mild metabolic acidosis. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying renal or hepatic pathology. Low HbA1c with glucosuria is unusual and may warrant further investigation. Recommend nephrology evaluation and assessment for possible diabetes, renal tubular disorders, or hepatic dysfunction.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
